preflight_quotes.arc_md: A three-state model — Seen / Interacted / Understood — is to be declared in a future `knowledge-state-substrate.md` design document.
preflight_quotes.ecology_substrate_md: Reserved API surface: when that doc lands, `emitObservation()` may, in addition, push a `Seen`-state entry for the species.
preflight_quotes.biology_substrate_md: Per arc.md §8.5, knowledge state is gated on `knowledge-state-substrate.md`. When that lands, biology may listen for "player has Understood concept X" to unlock advanced recipes / parametric ranges / ecosystem templates.
preflight_quotes.items_1_8_playbook: arc.md Section 8.5 explicitly gates this system: "NO system may consume knowledge-state queries before that design doc lands."
preflight_quotes.quests_js: Quest schema (versioned — add fields freely, never remove): id / title / description / giver / steps / reward — step types include 'dialogue' | 'inspect' | 'use_item' | 'quiz' | 'complete'.
preflight_quotes.items_js: Each item has: id - unique key used in inventory and save data; name; description; icon; category (tool / part / upgrade / quest); stackable.
preflight_quotes.saveSystem_js: Save shape (v1): { version: 1, player: { x, y, scene }, inventory: string[], completedQuests: string[], activeQuest: { id, stepIndex } | null, upgrades: string[], journal: string[], timestamp: ISO string } — current default also includes state.knowledge = { unlocked: [], discoveries: [] } and state.observations = [].

open_questions_enumerated: 13.1 Replace state.observations or layer on top
13.3 Confidence / mastery beyond Seen / Interacted / Understood
13.9 Cultural representation encoding in data structure
13.10 Cross-cutting open questions (knowledge x ecology x biology x world model)
notes.discovered_existing_implementation: src/renderer/game/systems/knowledgeSystem.js + data/knowledgeConcepts.js already implement a concept-unlock model with cascade prerequisites and Understood-state semantics. The substrate design layers on top of and generalizes the existing implementation rather than replacing it; section 9 (Save state model) specifies the migration path that preserves the legacy state.knowledge.unlocked / discoveries shape.
notes.next_step_recommended: User reviews the substrate document, resolves open questions (especially 13.1 layer-vs-replace, 13.7 data file layout, 13.9 cultural-content encoding), then a future dispatch implements the substrate per spec with the same-commit amendments to ecology-substrate.md, biology-substrate.md, and arc.md §8.5 named in the Cross-document drift resolution section.
